concerns.text: FR-010 (Real-world data extraction) and SC-007 are present in plan.md but have NO corresponding tasks. The spec.md explicitly limits scope to synthetic data and contains no US-4. Tasks for real-world data extraction, dataset loading, and evaluation on real attention maps are missing.

FR-001 requires generating 10,000 synthetic attention matrices (or trajectories per plan). T017b generates trajectories, but there is no explicit task to generate the full 10,000 count or to verify the dataset size matches the requirement. The task description mentions 'configurable number' but lacks a specific execution step to ensure the 10k target is met.

FR-008 requires computing a 'theoretical lower bound' based on a quantization noise model. T032 implements this, but the task description is vague ('analytical noise model') and does not explicitly mandate the derivation or implementation of the specific formula ($\Delta^2/12$) mentioned in the plan, risking an untestable success criterion (SC-002).

FR-002 requires training an MLP using FOUR moments (mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis). T022 defines the MLP with 4 inputs, but T010 (unit test) and T011 (unit test) only mention 'moment extraction' without specifying that the test must validate the extraction of ALL FOUR moments (skew/kurt) required by FR-002.

Task T035b references 'Spec Edge Cases' but does not explicitly tag the specific edge case from the spec (extreme outlier magnitudes) or the fallback mechanism (fallback to KVarN) in a way that allows direct traceability to the spec's Edge Cases section.

Task T017a introduces a 'DriftModel' class. While the plan.md justifies this as a methodological update, the spec.md US-1 only mentions 'controlled sparsity and outlier magnitudes' for static matrices. There is no task explicitly linking the 'temporal drift' implementation to a specific requirement in the spec, creating a traceability gap for this scope expansion.
